channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: More than 46,000 Palestinians have been killed in the Israel-Hamas war, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — More than 46,000 Palestinians have been killed in the Israel-Hamas war, Gaza's Health Ministry said Thursday, with no end in sight to the 15-month conflict. The ministry said a total of 46,006 Palestinians have been killed and 109,378 wounded.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Filipino Catholics pray for good health and peace in huge procession venerating Jesus statue
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Some 1.8 million mostly barefoot Catholic worshippers marched Thursday in an annual procession in the Philippines that venerates a centuries-old black statue of Jesus.
item.dc:creator: TERESA CEROJANO AND JOEAL CALUPITAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Scientists drill nearly 2 miles down to pull 1.2 million-year-old ice core from Antarctic
item.description: An international team of scientists announced Thursday they’ve successfully drilled one of the oldest ice cores yet, penetrating nearly 2 miles (2.8 kilometers) to Antarctic bedrock to reach ice they say is at least 1.2 million years old.
item.dc:creator: PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mozambique opposition leader returns from self-imposed exile as police fire tear gas at supporters
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique's main opposition leader returned from self-imposed exile on Thursday clutching a bible and saying he still rejects the results of a disputed election last year that has sparked more than two months of protests and a violent crackdown by security forces.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope asks aide to deliver his annual foreign policy address, tells ambassadors he's battling a cold
item.description: Pope Francis has told ambassadors accredited to the Holy See that he wasn’t up to delivering his annual foreign policy speech and asked an aide to deliver it instead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Attack on Chad's presidential palace leaves 19 dead
item.description: State media in Chad say an attack on the presidential palace left 18 assailants dead and six in custody, with one soldier killed and three wounded.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Japan's Ishiba heads to Malaysia and Indonesia to strengthen defense and economic ties
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba on Thursday began a trip to Malaysia and Indonesia as part of an effort to strengthen defense and economic ties with Southeast Asia as China's threats grow in the region.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scientists drill nearly 2 miles down to pull 1.2 million-year-old ice core from Antarctic
item.description: An international team of scientists say they’ve successfully drilled one of the oldest ice cores yet, penetrating nearly 2 miles to Antarctic bedrock to reach ice that's at least 1.2 million years old.
item.dc:creator: Paolo Santalucia, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venezuela's political newcomer Edmundo González says it's his turn to rule
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Edmundo González has become a beacon of hope for millions of Venezuelans. They want to call him president. He believes he won that office at the ballot box last year. The government of President Nicolás Maduro says he did not.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump provokes anxieties with Greenland, Panama Canal military talk
item.description: President-elect Trump’s refusal to rule out military action to attain his ambitions of acquiring the Panama Canal and Greenland has sparked some stern responses overseas and spurred a global debate over how seriously to take the incoming president.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Jeffries: 'Confused' by Trump's 'obsession' with Greenland
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) hammered President-elect Trump on Wednesday for what he characterized as an “obsession” with the idea of the U.S. taking over Greenland, accusing Trump of fixating on an issue of no consequence for working-class Americans.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Cold spell claims first two lives in El Paso-Juarez area
item.description: Tuesday’s death of a homeless man presumably due to hypothermia has set off alarms in a border city with a large floating population of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli Army says it recovered body of hostage in Gaza
item.description: The Israeli Army on Wednesday said it had recovered the body from the Gaza Strip of an Israeli citizen who was kidnapped by Hamas during its terrorist attack on Oct. 7 and held hostage, and was working to identify the remains of another body as a possible Israeli hostage.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: House Republican: Gulf of Mexico name less important than Trump's actions
item.description: Rep. Austin Scott (R-Ga.) said President-elect Trump’s musing about changing the name of the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America is less important than his actions in the region, particularly when it comes to curbing illegal fishing and drug trafficking.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers introduce ‘Georgian Nightmare Non-Recognition Act' in warning to Putin allied government
item.description: Senior Republican and Democratic representatives will introduce a bill on Wednesday prohibiting recognition of the Georgian Dream government amid daily protests against the new government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian journalist Cecilia Sala freed from detention in Iran
item.description: Italian journalist Cecilia Sala was released after being detained in Iran for three weeks. Sala's plight became intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer wanted by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis has named the first woman to head a major Vatican office, choosing an Italian nun
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis on Monday named the first woman to head a major Vatican office, appointing an Italian nun, Sister Simona Brambilla, to become prefect of the department responsible for all the Catholic Church's religious orders.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why Greenland? Remote but resource-rich island occupies a key position in a warming world
item.description: Remote, icy and mostly pristine, Greenland plays an outsized role in the daily weather experienced by billions of people and in the climate changes taking shape all over the planet.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader says his son-in-law has been kidnapped in Caracas
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González, who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election, said his son-in-law was kidnapped Tuesday in Venezuela's capital.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump reinforces 'all hell will break out' if hostages not returned by inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump once again threatened "all hell will break out" if the hostages in Gaza are not freed by the time he enters office, though he refused to detail what actions he will take.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kim Jong Un’s big guns spotted on Russian front lines: report
item.description: A North Korean howitzer has been reported on Russia's front lines following November sightings of a shipment of two M1989 Koksan self-propelled big guns as Pyongyang entered the war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Controversial founder of French far right, Jean-Marie Le Pen, dies aged 96
item.description: Jean-Marie Le Pen, who founded France's far-right National Front party, died at 96 years old. Le Pen was a controversial and confrontational figure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Law student killed by elephant during vacation to Thailand: officials
item.description: Blanca Ojanguren, a 22-year-old student from Spain, was killed by an elephant on Friday during a bathing session at Koh Yao Elephant Care
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Pierre Poilievre? Canada's Conservative leader seeking to become next prime minister after Trudeau exit
item.description: The leader of Canada's Conservative party, Pierre Poilievre, has been leading in the polls and is favorite to become the country's next prime minister following the exit of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to announce resignation: Reports
item.description: It remains unclear whether Justin Trudeau will leave immediately or stay on as prime minister until a new leader is selected, the report added.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation
